package_label: Vessel speed optimisation and route planning
package_description: Slow steaming and AI-assisted route planning for large international vessels. Fuel saving of 12% from speed optimisation (cube-law); applies to conventional HFO and LNG transition states. Well-documented in bulk carrier and container shipping operations.
rollout_limit_notes: National adoption bound reflecting international vessel fleet age (~25-30 yr), IMO decarbonisation targets, port infrastructure, and fuel supply chain development.
evidence_summary: Maersk/MSC slow steaming programmes; IMO MEPC EEDI/EEOI data; Cargill and Vale voyage optimisation reports.
derivation_method: Energy coefficients from AES 2025 Table F1 international maritime bunkers (29.5 PJ, 2023-24) / estimated service 200,000 million_tkm = 147.5 GJ/million_tkm. Fuel split: 60% HFO (88.5), 40% MDO (59.0). NGGI check: 11.4 tCO2e/million_tkm × 200,000 / 1e6 = 2.3 MtCO2e.
review_notes: Very well-documented; savings constrained by port scheduling and contract requirements.

package_label: Wind-assist technology (Flettner rotors and rigid sails)
package_description: Wind-assist propulsion via Flettner rotors or rigid sails reducing main engine fuel consumption by up to 8% on open-ocean routes. Commercially deployed on bulk carriers (Cargill, Vale). Applies to conventional HFO and LNG transition states.
rollout_limit_notes: National adoption bound reflecting international vessel fleet age (~25-30 yr), IMO decarbonisation targets, port infrastructure, and fuel supply chain development.
evidence_summary: Cargill Pyxis fleet wind-assist data; Vale wind-assist bulk carrier trials; Viking Line hybrid rotor sail performance; Norsepower Rotor Sail specifications.
derivation_method: Energy coefficients from AES 2025 Table F1 international maritime bunkers (29.5 PJ, 2023-24) / estimated service 200,000 million_tkm = 147.5 GJ/million_tkm. Fuel split: 60% HFO (88.5), 40% MDO (59.0). NGGI check: 11.4 tCO2e/million_tkm × 200,000 / 1e6 = 2.3 MtCO2e.
review_notes: Effective on routes with favourable wind patterns; not all Australian trade routes are optimal.
